jaguar_matlab_tyre.label: jaguar_matlab_tyre
jaguar_matlab_tyre.title: Jaguar MATLAB Tyre Tool
facts: Formula E Season 11 at Jaguar TCS Racing
New tyres and four-wheel drive in attack mode introduced significant changes
Team needed failsafe contingencies and wanted to unlock tyre performance
Branson proposed and developed an in-house MATLAB tool on the race weekend itself
Tool combined mechanical engineering (diploma background) with computer science skills
Tool quantified the friction-brake energy required to raise tyre core temperatures without compromising battery strategy
Validated the tool against race telemetry
Presented findings to engineers — findings supported strategy decisions
Contributed to holding off Porsche for P2 and securing a win at Shanghai Race 2
Tool still in use at Jaguar today
do_not_say: specific lap times or energy figures you do not know

work_experience_ref.note: Fact 3 (MATLAB tyre/RESS models), fact 12 (energy regen sensitivity analysis tool), fact 14 (battery temperature correlation with simulator)

jaguar_alkamel.label: jaguar_alkamel
jaguar_alkamel.title: Jaguar Alkamel Timing Feed Converter
facts: Jaguar TCS Racing internship; compact team — responsibilities overlapped
Developed a Python-based data pipeline converting raw Alkamel Timing Feed data from a noSQL database into telemetry channels
Sought feedback from Vehicle Performance Group to keep channels relevant
Collaborated with software engineers on CI/CD architecture design
Sought code reviews with senior engineers for OOP scalability (design patterns, modularity, extensive error handling)
Aligned with race strategists to ensure outputs had direct race impact
Started as a single Python file; grew into a fully functioning application
Application still powering performance at Jaguar today
do_not_say: any performance metrics you do not know

work_experience_ref.note: Fact 0 (FIA timing feed pipeline), fact 1 (worked within race data pipeline), fact 16 (NoSQL race data → ATLAS channels)

jaguar_youtube_cnn.label: jaguar_youtube_cnn
jaguar_youtube_cnn.title: Jaguar YouTube Clip CNN Extension
facts: Jaguar internship; regularly spoke with colleagues across departments
Discovered an unused internal repository that identified onboard clips from YouTube livestreams
Saw potential to repurpose and extend the tool
Extended it to recognise competitor dashboard footage showing key data such as tyre pressures — needed by the tyre department
Had no prior machine learning experience at the time
Studied the fundamentals of the existing CNN classifier independently
Sourced own training dataset
Innovation significantly eased manual burden — freed engineers to focus on performance-critical tasks
Demonstrates ownership and turning curiosity into tangible impact
do_not_say: specific frameworks beyond CNN unless you know them

work_experience_ref.note: Fact 4 (CNN and OpenCV image recognition tooling saving engineers time from manual video review)

gryphon_gps.label: gryphon_gps
gryphon_gps.title: Gryphon Racing GPS Sensor Bridge
facts: Leeds Gryphon Racing (university Formula Student team)
New ECU and data acquisition system introduced a gap: electrics team unsure which sensors to install and what channel strategies to use; vehicle dynamics team struggling to validate design choices
Branson identified the cross-team disconnect and facilitated a meeting between leads
Communicated each team's requirements and constraints clearly
Concluded that GPS sensors and wheel speed sensors were critical for correlating tyre models
Defined sampling rates, logging strategies, and channel strategies together
This provided vehicle dynamics team performance metrics in the format they needed
Turned initial confusion into a key vehicle data architecture decision that will support performance for years

fyp_marl_f1.label: fyp_marl_f1
fyp_marl_f1.title: Final Year Project — F1 Multi-Agent RL Simulator
facts: Undergraduate Computer Science final year project
Built a 100Hz F1 race simulator from scratch in Python — 100 full races complete in ~4.56 seconds (21.9 races/sec), providing the sample throughput required for RL training loops

Designed a pluggable multi-agent policy architecture (BaseAgent in src/agents.py) where any RL algorithm (DQN, PPO, MADDPG) can be swapped in via a single callable interface: (driver, race_state) → (RiskLevel, bool)

Modelled stochastic race dynamics: incident probability as a function of corner difficulty × risk multiplier, and overtake success as an independent stochastic process — making the environment non-trivially hard for deterministic policies

Grounded simulation parameters in real F1 telemetry: extracted overtake probability distributions by track position from 2023 Belgian Grand Prix data using FastF1, then mapped them to physical track geometry via GPS centreline processing (KML → Cartesian coordinates)
Implemented three baseline agent archetypes — Conservative, Aggressive, and Adaptive — with measurable outcome differences: Aggressive won 82/100 races vs. Adaptive's 18/100 in batch testing

Identified the core MARL challenge: the Adaptive agent's strategy is conditioned on the opponent's gap, creating non-stationary opponent behaviour — exactly the problem algorithms like MADDPG are designed to address
Built a modular, config-driven simulator (main.py, config.json) separating track loading, race state initialisation, and simulation execution — enabling systematic ablation studies
Ran an autonomous ML research pipeline (autoresearch) based on a custom GPT pretraining loop with MuonAdamW optimiser, logging experiments to results.tsv with val_bpb as the key metric
At 21.9 races/sec, a training run of 1 million episodes would complete in ~12.7 hours — practically feasible for overnight RL training
Targeted MARL algorithm comparison (DQN, PPO, MADDPG) as the stated MVP objective for benchmarking against the stochastic racing environment

Simulator is designed to be incrementally extended — planned complexity additions include tyre degradation modelling and pit stop strategy, allowing direct comparison of how different RL algorithms adapt as the environment becomes more complex
do_not_say: claim the RL algorithms were fully trained or benchmarked unless you know the outcome
specific loss figures or reward curves beyond what is listed above
imply the autoresearch pipeline relates to the F1 simulator — it is a separate GPT pretraining experiment

travelindr_mvp.label: travelindr_mvp
travelindr_mvp.title: Travelindr MVP / A-B Testing
facts: Co-founder and CEO of Travelindr — a group travel-itinerary generator
Built in a cross-functional team using Agile ways of working
Ran focus-group discussions to inform product direction
Created UI mockups to validate design decisions
Conducted A/B testing and user interviews to iterate on features
Set product strategy and direction
Progressed the product to MVP stage
Pitched to investors; secured incubation within a multinational company
do_not_say: imply Branson wrote production code — the role was executive/product
